body.u: I left school at eighteen and joined the W.R.N.S I visited Gibraltar, Germany, Hongkong and Cyprus and served at least in each port. It was a great life. By the time I was 27 I was a captain. After serving 10 yearsyears' service left to concentrate on my family and personal side of life. With twins it was a lot coping but I managed. I missed the service it was so friendly and so many different jobs to do you couldn’t get bored.

I managed to get a part-time job when the children started school but when they got older I worked full time and got a good job as an acocuntantaccountant for a shop. The training in the W.R.N.S had come in handy.
The children left school with a handful of O and A levels and a journalist.

We had a family reunion on my 25th wedding aniversaryanniversary. I was 47 my husband 49. We both carried on with our jobs. I helped to run a youth club and my husband Mark ran an athletic club. One of the girls got married to a doctor at the same hospital as she worked. It was just a family affair. When the kids left home we looked around for a smaller more modern house. We ended up having one built.

My husband started a firm off which did very well. Then one of the girls got the sack the the journalist. Her and her common law husband had an accident in their car nothing serious. The nurse had a boy then a girl. The other finalyfinally got married.
I retired and became involved with the nursrynursery school I did child minding. We had an extension built on at the back of our house.
My husband retired through ill health and died shortly afterwards. I then got involved with the church.
